svg.title: RDMA control plane vs data plane
svg.desc: RDMA splits work into two planes. Control plane (setup, infrequent) goes through kernel: memory registration, queue pair creation, connection setup. Data plane (every send, hot path) bypasses kernel entirely: NIC handles reliability, flow control, packetization in hardware.
svg.text: Where does the kernel's old work go?
Split into control plane (setup, kernel) and data plane (transfer, NIC)
Control plane runs once at setup; data plane runs millions of times
Setup overhead is amortized — that's why RDMA wins on sustained traffic, not one-shot calls
Kernel: still owns memory protection setup, page pinning, fabric config — just not per-packet decisions
